Date: 2025-08-20T16:45:34.255Z
Electron: 37.2.3
ElectronBuildId: 12035395
Chromium: 138.0.7204.100
Node.js: 22.17.0
V8: 13.8.500258-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
- OS Version: windows 11 

Steps to Reproduce:
The issue is intermittent. I performed a bisect and confirmed that it is caused by the Copilot extension.

The scenario occurs when you press Ctrl+X at the same moment Copilot provides a suggestion. When this happens, the selected code is deleted, but the clipboard remains empty.

Consequently, when you move to another line and try to paste, nothing happens. Undoing with Ctrl+Z and repeating the cut operation doesn't fix it.

The workaround is to switch to a different editor tab and then return. After doing so, Ctrl+X works as expected. This suggests the race condition might be corrupting a temporary cache or state within VS Code.

A keyboard shortcut configuration in VS Code is also known as a keyboard rule . Each rule consists of the following attributes:

- key : describes the pressed key(s), for example ⌘F (Windows, Linux Ctrl+F ) .
- command : the identifier of the VS Code command to execute, for example workbench.view.explorer to open the Explorer view.
- when : (optional) clause containing a boolean expression that is evaluated depending on the current context .

- key : describes the pressed key(s), for example ⌘F (Windows, Linux Ctrl+F ) .

Code/Command:
key

- command : the identifier of the VS Code command to execute, for example workbench.view.explorer to open the Explorer view.

Code/Command:
command

Code/Command:
workbench.view.explorer

- when : (optional) clause containing a boolean expression that is evaluated depending on the current context .

Code/Command:
when

Chords (two separate keypress actions) are described by separating the two keypresses with a space. For example, Ctrl+K Ctrl+C .

When a key is pressed, the following evaluation rules are applied:

- Rules are evaluated from bottom to top .
- The first rule that matches both the key and when clause, is accepted.
- If a rule is found, no more rules are processed.
- If a rule is found and has a command set, the command is executed.

- Rules are evaluated from bottom to top .

- The first rule that matches both the key and when clause, is accepted.

Code/Command:
key

Code/Command:
when

- If a rule is found, no more rules are processed.

- If a rule is found and has a command set, the command is executed.

Code/Command:
command

Code/Command:
command

The additional keybindings.json rules are appended at runtime to the bottom of the default rules, thus allowing them to overwrite the default rules. The keybindings.json file is watched by VS Code, so editing it while VS Code is running will update the rules at run-time.

Code/Command:
keybindings.json

Code/Command:
keybindings.json

[bug, verified, insiders-released, inline-chat] Copilot "Quick Actions" missing since one of the latest updates Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes (all other extensions are disabled) - VS Code Version: 1.111.0 - OS Version: Windows 11 and Arch Linux Steps to Reproduce: 1. Disabled all other extensions including Github Copilot Chat (or install a fresh vscode instance). 2. Enable for example rust-analyzer or anything that marks errors in the sourcecode (testet also with javascript files/lints) 3. Hover over the error. You can see "✨ Fix" to invoke copilot: 4. Clicking "Fix" doesn't do anything 5. Install "Github Copilot Chat" (current version 0.39.0) 6. Go back to the error and hover over it. "✨ Fix" is gone. 7. Restarting doesn't help.

retrieved_list:
[bug, *out-of-scope, chat] Chat Widget does not follow display language changes correctly

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.95.3 (Universal)
- OS Version: macOS 15.1.1 (24B91)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the Copilot Chat side panel.
2. Change VSCode language via `Configure Display Language` command. Confirm `Restart` button.
3. VScode restarts and successfully switches to new language. Copilot Chat still displaying with old language.
4. Reload VSCode via `Developer: Reload Window` command. (This step must be performed after Copilot Chat has finished initializing.)
5. Copilot Chat switches to new language successfully.

P.S. `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded` cache is likely the culprit. It is called before `viewModel` exists.

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/05fd1308240072efb09749954fd478221fd01d55/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatWidget.ts#L563-L582

Start another chat (Chat B) and stay in that chat 3. Wait until side bar shows that Chat A "Input needed" 4. Open Chat A 5. See that there's nothing to give input on

Version: 1.103.2 (Universal) Commit: 6f17636121051a53c88d3e605c491d22af2ba755 Date: 2025-08-20T16:45:34.255Z Electron: 37.2.3 ElectronBuildId: 12035395 Chromium: 138.0.7204.100 Node.js: 22.17.0 V8: 13.8.500258-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 24.5.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Use VSCode with multiple window open. 2. Allow MacOS to sleep 3. Wake up MacOS and navigate to VSCode 4. VSCode intermittently will completely fail to render - Every window Rendering completely broken: Sorry this bug report is pretty sparse but I'm not able to use the help tools built into vscode to make a report. This rendering issue happened once or twice in the previous version of VSCode, but since the most recent update it is failing more often and requires a restart everytime. I do know that I haven't added any extensions in a while. Definitely in the last couple of updates. If you have any suggestions on being able to get some diagnostic information via a terminal command or by setting a flag in VSCode I am happy to do so and to continue to update this issue. Unfortunately due to the nature of this bug I can't disable plugins as I am relying on them day to day.

[bug, help wanted, *not-reproducible] Continuity copy/pasting rich text from iOS to macOS does not work properly in the editor

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.91.1
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 23.5.0

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Copy text from Safari on an iOS device
2. Paste the text into a VSCode instance running on macOS (with the same iCloud account and continuity features enabled)

### Expected Result

The editor should paste a plain text version of the copied data.

### Actual Result

Instead of pasting a plain text version of the data, the editor inserts a string like this:

```
/var/folders/bp/fvm0x9c92xb3x02w8qsg21jh0000gn/T/com.apple.useractivityd/shared-pasteboard/items/AC079EB3-C47A-48B3-95DC-C03990D76EF1/42514c636c7fe381d40102cae8877a9c82f0aeb8.rtfd
```

### Video Demo

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->

## VS Code Version
1.110.0 (February 2026)

## OS
macOS (Apple Silicon / ARM64) - 15.7.3

## Steps to Reproduce

### Bug 1: Caps Lock inserts symbols in integrated terminal
1. Configure Caps Lock as input source switcher (RU/EN) via System Settings → Keyboard → Input Sources
2. Open VS Code integrated terminal
3. Launch any CLI app (e.g. OpenCode)
4. Press Caps Lock to switch keyboard layout

**Expected:** Layout switches silently, no characters inserted 
**Actual:** Strange symbols / empty box characters are inserted into the terminal input

### Bug 2: Cmd+Q does not quit VS Code
1. Open VS Code 1.110
2. Press Cmd+Q

**Expected:** VS Code quits 
**Actual:** VS Code does not quit or hangs in background

## Additional Context

- Both bugs are **not reproducible in VS Code 1.109** and **1.100** — confirmed by rollback testing
- Both bugs are **not reproducible in VSCodium 1.110** (same codebase, without Microsoft-specific patches)
- Both bugs are **not reproducible in iTerm2** running the same CLI apps
- This strongly suggests the regression is introduced by Microsoft-specific patches/telemetry layer in 1.110, not in the open-source Code-OSS base

## Configuration
```json
"terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration": "off",
"terminal.integrated.setLocaleVariables": true,
"keyboard.dispatch": "keyCode"

To troubleshoot keyboard shortcut problems, you can execute the command Developer: Toggle Keyboard Shortcuts Troubleshooting . This activates logging of dispatched keyboard shortcuts and opens the Output panel with the corresponding log file.

You can then press your desired keyboard shortcut and check what keyboard shortcut VS Code detects and what command is invoked.

For example, when pressing cmd+/ in a code editor on macOS, the logging output would be:

Code/Command:
cmd+/

Code/Command:
[KeybindingService]: / Received keydown event - modifiers: [meta], code: MetaLeft, keyCode: 91, key: Meta
[KeybindingService]: | Converted keydown event - modifiers: [meta], code: MetaLeft, keyCode: 57 ('Meta')
[KeybindingService]: \ Keyboard event cannot be dispatched.
[KeybindingService]: / Received keydown event - modifiers: [meta], code: Slash, keyCode: 191, key: /
[KeybindingService]: | Converted keydown event - modifiers: [meta], code: Slash, keyCode: 85 ('/')
[KeybindingService]: | Resolving meta+[Slash]
[KeybindingService]: \ From 2 keybinding entries, matched editor.action.commentLine, when: editorTextFocus && !editorReadonly, source: built-in.

In the example log, the first keydown event is for the MetaLeft key ( cmd ) and cannot be dispatched. The second keydown event is for the Slash key ( / ) and is dispatched as meta+[Slash] . There were two keyboard shortcut entries mapped from meta+[Slash] and the one that matched was for the command editor.action.commentLine , which has the when condition editorTextFocus && !editorReadonly and is a built-in keyboard shortcut entry.

- VS Code Version: 1.95.3 (Universal)
- OS Version: macOS 15.1.1 (24B91)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the Copilot Chat side panel.
2. Change VSCode language via `Configure Display Language` command. Confirm `Restart` button.
3. VScode restarts and successfully switches to new language. Copilot Chat still displaying with old language.
4. Reload VSCode via `Developer: Reload Window` command. (This step must be performed after Copilot Chat has finished initializing.)
5. Copilot Chat switches to new language successfully.

P.S. `renderWelcomeViewContentIfNeeded` cache is likely the culprit. It is called before `viewModel` exists.

[bug, verified, candidate, chat] Search subagent shows up in tool picker when it's disabled by setting

The search subagent should be hidden in VS Code when its setting is disabled, not just disabled in the agent's list at runtime. Do this by using a when clause on the tool contribution in package.json

Forked from https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/291154

[bug, on-testplan, insiders-released, chat-subagents] Agents that are called as SubAgents won't have access to the 'agent' tool anymore Type: Bug If I create a subAgent and inside that subAgent I don't have access to the `runSubAgent` tool anymore. I am creating a subAgent from my main chat window and the subAgents don't have the 'agent' tool access to them. I remember creating subAgents within SubAgents with the 'runSubAgent' tool and for some reason now the tool itself is not getting listed in any subAgents and all my subAgents complain they don't know how to call the tool and how to make the 'runSubAgent' tool call and going haywire. I had to spend hours and hours of various trials and combinations only to feel like its a bug and its a serious bug of course. Also, I have the "Cutom Agent in Subagent" enabled. I used almost 10 different models including Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 flash, Gemini 3 Pro. I made almost 50+ premium requests just to test it. **Test Setup 1 using Prompt** 1. I have a `/dispatch` prompt that takes a request and it has access to the `agent` tool - It can happily create subAgents. 2. The `/dispatch` will spawn the `dispatcher` subAgent and this is completely blocked as it doesn't have access to `agent` tool. Not just this agent, I have so many other agents that doesn't have the `agent` tool. **Test Setup 2 using agent** 1. I am not using prompt file and now directly used the `dispatcher` agent and now it has `agent` tool in it as expected. 2. I am creating subAgent `codeSearcher` and now that has the same issue, it doesn't have access to `agent` tool too. I tested using the latest VS Code insiders and also one version before it also has the same issues. ```markdown # .github/agents/dispatcherAgent.agent.md name: dispatcherAgent description: Dispatch control plane -- classify, route, filter, evaluate tools: ['execute/getTerminalOutput', 'execute/runInTerminal', 'read/terminalSelection', 'read/terminalLastCommand', 'edit', 'agent'] target: vscode ``` ```markdown Metadata requestType : ChatCompletions model : claude-sonnet-4.5 maxPromptTokens : 127997 maxResponseTokens: 16000 location : 7 otherOptions : {"temperature":0,"stream":true} intent : undefined startTime : 2025-12-19T11:54:44.066Z endTime : 2025-12-19T11:55:01.070Z duration : 17004ms ourRequestId : f5327b5b-9a42-4f65-b619-b2b85227c673 requestId : f5327b5b-9a42-4f65-b619-b2b85227c673 serverRequestId : f5327b5b-9a42-4f65-b619-b2b85227c673 timeToFirstToken : 2133ms resolved model : claude-sonnet-4.5 usage : {"completion_tokens":823,"prompt_tokens":12786,"prompt_tokens_details":{"cached_tokens":0},"total_tokens":13609} tools : [ { "function": { "name": "create_directory", "description": "Create a new directory structure in the workspace. title: Copilot CLI : MCP tools not honored in Custom Agents
user_feedback: [Problem] Custom agents sub-agents lose declared tools when run as third-party agents inside VS Code; [Symptoms] sub-agent reports only file-viewing tools, ignoring tools property; [Environment] VS Code Insiders 1.113.0-insider on Linux x64 (WSL2); [Possible Category] Extensions; [Problem Type] Tool Access Restriction
retrieved_list: [bug, on-testplan, insiders-released, chat-subagents] Agents that are called as SubAgents won't have access to the 'agent' tool anymore Type: Bug If I create a subAgent and inside that subAgent I don't have access to the `runSubAgent` tool anymore. I am creating a subAgent from my main chat window and the subAgents don't have the 'agent' tool access to them. I remember creating subAgents within SubAgents with the 'runSubAgent' tool and for some reason now the tool itself is not getting listed in any subAgents and all my subAgents complain they don't know how to call the tool and how to make the 'runSubAgent' tool call and going haywire. I had to spend hours and hours of various trials and combinations only to feel like its a bug and its a serious bug of course. Also, I have the "Cutom Agent in Subagent" enabled. I used almost 10 different models including Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 flash, Gemini 3 Pro. I made almost 50+ premium requests just to test it. **Test Setup 1 using Prompt** 1. I have a `/dispatch` prompt that takes a request and it has access to the `agent` tool - It can happily create subAgents. 2. The `/dispatch` will spawn the `dispatcher` subAgent and this is completely blocked as it doesn't have access to `agent` tool. Not just this agent, I have so many other agents that doesn't have the `agent` tool. **Test Setup 2 using agent** 1. I am not using prompt file and now directly used the `dispatcher` agent and now it has `agent` tool in it as expected. 2. I am creating subAgent `codeSearcher` and now that has the same issue, it doesn't have access to `agent` tool too. title: UNRESPONSIVE extension host: github.copilot-chat took 98% of 4948ms causing other extension to hang
user_feedback: [Problem] Unresponsive extension host due to github.copilot-chat hogging CPU, [Symptoms] causes other extensions like Modus Toolbox Assistant to hang while fetching manifest data and shows high CPU usage warnings, [Environment] Windows 11, VS Code 1.120.0, [Possible Category] Performance, [Problem Type] Extension resource hogging.
retrieved_list:
[bug, extension-host, perf, chat, ai-triaged] VS Code becomes extremely slow when GitHub Copilot Chat is active (extension host lag + uncaught error)

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

* VS Code Version: 1.XX.X (latest stable at time of report)
* OS Version: Windows 11 x64

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code normally with extensions enabled.
2. Ensure GitHub Copilot Chat is installed (default Copilot extension in current versions).
3. Start typing in any TypeScript or JavaScript file.
4. Editor becomes extremely slow: typing delay, save delay, and general UI lag.
5. Open **Developer: Show Running Extensions** and observe activation times.

Observed Behavior:

* GitHub Copilot Chat shows:

 * Activation time ~2000–2300 ms
 * "1 uncaught error" in Running Extensions panel.
* Extension host becomes slow.
* Typing latency can reach several seconds.
* Saving files sometimes takes minutes.
* Issue occurs even with a minimal extension set.

Additional Observations:

* Disabling **GitHub Copilot Chat** removes the lag and restores normal editor performance.
* The issue appears related to the Copilot Chat extension throwing an internal error which blocks the extension host.
* Other extensions show normal activation times.

Expected Behavior:

* Typing and saving should remain responsive even when Copilot is active.
* Copilot should not block the extension host when internal errors occur.

Notes:

* Issue reproduced without opening a large workspace.
* The slowdown is noticeable even in a normal VS Code window.
* Running extensions panel reports an uncaught error from Copilot Chat.

For large repos (>100 GB), opening up VSCode from root diretcory, search a question with `#codebase` tag in copilot chat, the extension host keeps restarting and clear out my questions. I've also seen the notification about Extension host unexpectedly terminated: I have disabled all the extensions except for the Github copilot and Github copilot chat extensions. [bug, freeze-slow-crash-leak] Copilot Extension Triggers CPU Burn via Infinite quickSort (Function NU) — Crashes Extension Host

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- **GitHub Copilot Extension Version**: 1.323.1586 (happens with release and pre-release)
- **VS Code Version**: 1.90.0-insider (happens with stable version too)
- **OS Version**: Windows 11 x64 (10.0.26120 Build 26120)

- **Logs**: See attached `.cpuprofile` and flamegraph screenshots. No specific logs in Output/Developer Console beyond high CPU usage warnings.

---

### Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code Insiders with **only GitHub Copilot enabled**.
2. Open any basic `.py` or `.js` file and begin typing (even minimal interaction triggers it).
3. Observe repeated **extension host unresponsiveness**. 
4. View `.cpuprofile` (auto-saved by VS Code) — shows 90%+ CPU usage from minified `quickSort` function (`NU`) inside `extension.js`.
5. Extension host becomes responsive again only after sorting ends or timeout is hit.

---

### Problem Description:

The Copilot extension consistently causes the extension host to hang due to pathological CPU usage in a minified recursive function named `NU` (aliased as `jle.quickSort`) found in `extension.js`. This function appears to be recursively sorting a large or malformed array, leading to severe performance degradation.

**Key Evidence**:

- `.cpuprofile` clearly shows `NU` repeatedly calling itself (`NU → NU → NU...`) for up to 20+ levels deep.
- Total sort times often exceed 10 seconds.
- The call originates from: 
 `c:/Users/frits/.vscode-insiders/extensions/github.copilot-1.323.1586/dist/extension.js:1`
- A snippet of the relevant minified code:
```js
function NU(e,t,n,a){
 if(n<a){
 var i=VUe(n,a),o=n-1;
 LU(e,i,a);
 for(var s=e[a],c=n;c<a;c++) t(e[c],s)<=0&&(o+=1,LU(e,o,c));
 LU(e,o+1,c);
 var l=o+1;
 NU(e,t,n,l-1), NU(e,t,l+1,a)
 }
}
jle.quickSort = function(e,t){ NU(e,t,0,e.length-1) }
```

---

### Why This Matters:

- This is not an isolated lag spike—it causes total **extension host lockup**.
- If Copilot is fetching completions or telemetry that trigger this, it’s likely **sorting malformed/duplicated/unbounded arrays**.
- Users lose all IntelliSense, completions, and LSP responsiveness for several seconds repeatedly.
- The issue reproduces *every time* Copilot is active.

---

### Attachments:

- `.cpuprofile` file (can upload on request)
- Multiple screenshots showing recursion depth and function call dominance
- Willing to test any patch or internal build that changes sorting strategy

---

Please confirm if more logs or traces would help. Happy to isolate this in a clean environment or try older builds if needed.

- VS Code Version: 1.110.0-insider (0366405821955682155a4507a122611c742df3fa)
- OS Version: macOS 26.1 (Build 25B78)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the integrated terminal in VS Code Insiders.
2. Use fish as the integrated terminal shell (fish at `/opt/homebrew/bin/fish`).
3. Run `sleep 100` (also seen with `top`).
4. Press Ctrl+C.

Expected:

`sleep` is interrupted (SIGINT) and the shell prompt returns.

Actual:

The process keeps running, and the terminal prints something like `[99;5u` (sometimes repeated).

Additional Information:

- Only reproduces with fish (fish 4.4.0). If I switch the integrated terminal profile to zsh, Ctrl+C works normally.
- Outside VS Code, Ctrl+C interrupts as expected in fish (Ghostty and macOS Terminal).
- `$TERM`:
 - VS Code: `xterm-256color`
 - Ghostty: `xterm-ghostty`
 - macOS Terminal: `xterm-256color` (Ctrl+C works)
- Ctrl+C also does nothing at the fish prompt (no running command).
- Disabling shell integration (`terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled: false`) does not change the behavior.
- Reproducible with `fish --no-config`.
- `fish_key_reader` prints `bind ctrl-c 'do something'` in both VS Code and Ghostty.
- `[99;5u` looks like a kitty keyboard protocol "CSI u" sequence for Ctrl+C.

- VS Code Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update to latest VSCode Insiders version within VSCode Insiders.
2. Restart Windows after update.
3. Right-click in a folder, notice there is no context menu option still.

I also tried reinstalling the VSCode Insiders (System) installation, I ensured the options to add the context menu options to explorer were enabled and completed the installation. To give the best chance of working, I rebooted again and then started VSCode. Again, right-clicking in a folder shows no context menu option for 'Open with VSCode' still.

I am making a new issue as the previous tracked ones are closed/locked for some reason.

- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/287832
- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/260389

_**Note:** The old context menu entry is also missing. I have no means to open VSCode from any right-click menu in Windows currently._

## Full About Info

```
Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
Commit: b074b32e5a00d95510b5471393a62c9be38c127e
Date: 2026-01-26T17:05:31.575Z
Electron: 39.2.7
ElectronBuildId: 13098910
Chromium: 142.0.7444.235
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
```

## Workaround

At this point in time, this feature has been broken for nearly 3 years. For those that do not want to continue to wait for a fix, this post contains a workaround that is currently working:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/204696#issuecomment-3720762337

I just installed this and it restored the menu option without issue.

[bug, verified, accessibility, insiders-released] Task Completed accessibility signal does not play if there are warnings

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **Yes**

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.98.2
- OS Version: Windows 11, v10.0.22631

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Enable accessibility.signals.taskCompleted
2. Run a task that only has info or warning problems
3. No sound plays upon completion

In `taskTerminalStatus.ts:97` you can see that we play the success audio cue if the exit code was 0 AND the number of problemMatcher matches was 0. We'll play the failure audio cue if the exit code was not 0 or there was an error.

**But** if the task exited with code 0 and warnings or info markers, it will fall into one of the final two else-ifs and no cue will play.

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/a4089e4ff0518295298917db2a79fbc21136511b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/tasks/browser/taskTerminalStatus.ts#L97

[bug, verified, chat-agent] Copilot tool `copilot_listCodeUsages`/`#usages` is essentially broken (with fix)

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: (latest built from code)
Version: 1.104.0-insider (system setup)
Commit: 5d24d0fea3ffc65b824989dda916714a46ca97a7
Date: 2025-08-19T05:03:18.690Z
Electron: 37.2.3
ElectronBuildId: 12035395
Chromium: 138.0.7204.100
Node.js: 22.17.0
V8: 13.8.500258-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

I noticed that the "#usages" tool usually doesn't work, almost always outputting "Symbol XYZ cannot be found" in chat (an apparent "error"). Similarly, leveraging the Language Model APIs directly, scenarios that involve the AI choosing `copilot_listCodeUsages` don't seem to work.

I tracked this down to the implementation in `usagesTool.tsx`. It looks like, if a file path is specified (which would be by default in Copilot Chat, when there is an active file, and also seems to be the case when AI leverages the tool), it does a `getDocumentSymbol` (i.e. the 'outline' symbols). Upon not finding a match (and there are several reasons why no match would be found), and since `getDefinitions`/`getReferences`/`getImplementations` requires an actual location to look up, it gives up.

A potential fix (which I've applied myself and seems to work well) is for `GetUsagesTool` to fall back to `getWorkspaceSymbols` (which it would have used if no file had been specified) and then retry `getDocumentSymbols` with those additional paths. With that fix, the tool seems to be functioning as expected, and leveraging the C++ language service for `getDefinitions`/`getReferences`/`getImplementations`.

- VS Code Version: 1.84.2
- OS Version: Windows 10 22H2 (Build 19045.3693)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open New Window of VS Code
2. Set the Activity Bar Position to "Top"
3. Enter Zen Mode (Command Pallete -> View: Toggle Zen Mode)
4. Title Bar should still be there

This was tested on the latest version of VS Code Insider and Results were the same and with all Extensions Off

Expected Behavior: 
Title Bar should be hidden (at least when in Zen Mode)

Possible Fix/Alternatives:
1. Hide it in Zen Mode

[bug, verified, insiders-released, chat, chat-tools] Cancellation token does not fire for Language Model Tools

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.95.0
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a new `yo code` hello world VS Code extension.
2. Add this to `package.json`

```json
 "languageModelTools": [
 {
 "name": "lmtools-cancel",
 "canBeReferencedInPrompt": true,
 "displayName": "Cancellation test",
 "modelDescription": "Cancellation test",
 "toolReferenceName": "cancel",
 "userDescription": "Cancellation test"
 }
 ]
```

3. Add this to `extension.ts`

```ts
class CancelLmTool implements vscode.LanguageModelTool<void> {
 async invoke(options: vscode.LanguageModelToolInvocationOptions<void>, token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<vscode.LanguageModelToolResult> {
 await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 10000));
 console.log(`LM Tool Cancellation state: ${token.isCancellationRequested}`);
 return new vscode.LanguageModelToolResult([new vscode.LanguageModelTextPart("dummy response")]);
 }
}

vscode.lm.registerTool('lmtools-cancel', new CancelLmTool());
```

4. Launch the extension and type `#cancel` in Copilot Chat. Click the cancel button before the 10 second timeout expires.
5. Note that `LM Tool Cancellation state: false` appears in the Debug Console, indicating that the cancellation token never actually fired despite appearing to cancel in the UI.

cc @lukka @microsoft/cpp-copilot

[bug, verified, accessibility, insiders-released] Model picker exposes checked checkbox state to screen readers but is not actionable ### Accessibility issue The model picker exposes a checked/checkbox state to screen readers even though the control is not actionable as a checkbox. ### Expected behavior The model picker should expose a semantic role/state that matches its actual interaction model (for example, menuitemradio or option with selected state), and avoid announcing "checkbox" when users cannot toggle it as a checkbox. ### Actual behavior Screen readers announce checked checkbox semantics that are misleading and not actionable.

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update to latest VSCode Insiders version within VSCode Insiders.
2. Restart Windows after update.
3. Right-click in a folder, notice there is no context menu option still.

I also tried reinstalling the VSCode Insiders (System) installation, I ensured the options to add the context menu options to explorer were enabled and completed the installation. To give the best chance of working, I rebooted again and then started VSCode. Again, right-clicking in a folder shows no context menu option for 'Open with VSCode' still.

I am making a new issue as the previous tracked ones are closed/locked for some reason.

- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/287832
- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/260389

_**Note:** The old context menu entry is also missing. I have no means to open VSCode from any right-click menu in Windows currently._

## Full About Info

```
Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
Commit: b074b32e5a00d95510b5471393a62c9be38c127e
Date: 2026-01-26T17:05:31.575Z
Electron: 39.2.7
ElectronBuildId: 13098910
Chromium: 142.0.7444.235
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
```

## Workaround

At this point in time, this feature has been broken for nearly 3 years. For those that do not want to continue to wait for a fix, this post contains a workaround that is currently working:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/204696#issuecomment-3720762337

I just installed this and it restored the menu option without issue.

[bug, verified, insiders-released, image-carousel] Image duplicated in carousel Testing #301610 - Experiment with images in chat, including generating 2 screenshots via Playwright MCP and adding 1 image to a new `images` folder in my repo manually - Start a new chat and ask `tell me more about any images in this repo` - Chat response lists the 3 images I'd expect: - The first (`image.png`) is the manual screenshot image I added to my repo - The second and third are both screenshots Playwright took of the VS Code website - - I click on one of the image links in the chat response - ❓ There are 4 images in the carousel- the first image appears to be unexpectedly duplicated -

[bug, macos, dialogs] Delete Dialogue box not proper visually Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.102.2 (Universal) - OS Version: Mac OS 26 Public Beta 1 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Delete a file or folder in explorer view 2. Can't see the select area in Do not ask again

[bug, upstream, verified, macos, electron, insiders-released] Rendering broken on macOS Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Unknown - issue is intermittent and difficult to replicate consistently. Version: 1.103.2 (Universal) Commit: 6f17636121051a53c88d3e605c491d22af2ba755 Date: 2025-08-20T16:45:34.255Z Electron: 37.2.3 ElectronBuildId: 12035395 Chromium: 138.0.7204.100 Node.js: 22.17.0 V8: 13.8.500258-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 24.5.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Use VSCode with multiple window open. 2. Allow MacOS to sleep 3. Wake up MacOS and navigate to VSCode 4. VSCode intermittently will completely fail to render - Every window Rendering completely broken: Sorry this bug report is pretty sparse but I'm not able to use the help tools built into vscode to make a report. This rendering issue happened once or twice in the previous version of VSCode, but since the most recent update it is failing more often and requires a restart everytime. I do know that I haven't added any extensions in a while. Definitely in the last couple of updates. If you have any suggestions on being able to get some diagnostic information via a terminal command or by setting a flag in VSCode I am happy to do so and to continue to update this issue. Unfortunately due to the nature of this bug I can't disable plugins as I am relying on them day to day.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->

- VS Code Version: VS Code desktop 1.83.1
- OS Version:
```
$ cat /etc/linuxmint/info
RELEASE=20.2
CODENAME=uma
EDITION="Cinnamon"
DESCRIPTION="Linux Mint 20.2 Uma"
DESKTOP=Gnome
TOOLKIT=GTK
NEW_FEATURES_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/rel_uma_cinnamon_whatsnew.php
RELEASE_NOTES_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/rel_uma_cinnamon.php
USER_GUIDE_URL=https://www.linuxmint.com/documentation.php
GRUB_TITLE=Linux Mint 20.2 Cinnamon
```

I managed to reproduce it on VS Code desktop 1.83.1

![Screenshot from 2023-10-16 14-55-49](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/1655894/117aac88-9d80-4ae7-b7ca-4b086d4ef80a)

To reproduce the bug I:
1. mounted a high-latency volume from my mobile to my home (Linux mint) with 
```
sudo curlftpfs -o allow_other vetal:vetal@192.168.71.101:2221 /home/vitaliy/ftp_phone/
```

2. cloned a sample repository https://github.com/vitaliy-guliy/recommended-extensions-sample to mounted folder.
To prevent caching I unmounted the volume and then mounted it again.

3. Opened terminal, navigated to `/home/vitaliy/ftp_phone/recommended-extensions-sample` and launched VS Code with `code .`

4. Opened extensions view, ensured all the recommendations were displayed and waited for starting the installation.

At this step, all the extensions are expected to be installed.

**NOTE:** This bug is not reproducible constantly.
I managed to reproduce it every once in a while. I need to unmount the volume, remove `~/.vscode` and start from the beginning.

See the VS Code logs below

![Screenshot from 2023-10-16 16-20-01](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/1655894/80b67435-728e-4223-bb2d-bd1d77f7edd4)

![Screenshot from 2023-10-16 16-19-52](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/1655894/dade207d-3835-436d-9ed9-229ffd0edbd5)

<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update to latest VSCode Insiders version within VSCode Insiders.
2. Restart Windows after update.
3. Right-click in a folder, notice there is no context menu option still.

I also tried reinstalling the VSCode Insiders (System) installation, I ensured the options to add the context menu options to explorer were enabled and completed the installation. To give the best chance of working, I rebooted again and then started VSCode. Again, right-clicking in a folder shows no context menu option for 'Open with VSCode' still.

I am making a new issue as the previous tracked ones are closed/locked for some reason.

- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/287832
- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/260389

_**Note:** The old context menu entry is also missing. I have no means to open VSCode from any right-click menu in Windows currently._

```

Node.js: 22.21.1

```

## Workaround

At this point in time, this feature has been broken for nearly 3 years. For those that do not want to continue to wait for a fix, this post contains a workaround that is currently working:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/204696#issuecomment-3720762337

I just installed this and it restored the menu option without issue.

[bug, verified, menus, insiders-released] Context menu in editor shows no scrollbar

The main menu entries have a scrollbar when needed:

![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/900690/d788be64-6014-47ae-b635-64276c7693fe)

The custom context menu has none, even when needed:

![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/900690/1cf78aa9-081d-4907-9f31-5164335abeaf)

Looking at the sources, I see that the menu sets it to visible though:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/be900a4b816a6cf83f3413f99bf6dcdd447ad7de/src/vs/base/browser/ui/menu/menu.ts#L237

[bug, important, verified, insiders-released] Custom context menus don't appear

Not sure why this doesn't happen on Insiders for me. Context menus don't appear on Windows where native context menus aren't used.

But the call stack I see is:

1. Showing a context menu fires an event https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/5eb1fa759598db6c69f39331506e7222aecf59e5/src/vs/platform/contextview/browser/contextMenuService.ts#L69
1. This is listened to by the hover service https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/5eb1fa759598db6c69f39331506e7222aecf59e5/src/vs/workbench/services/hover/browser/hoverService.ts#L40 and calls doHideHover
1. doHideHover calls `_contextViewService.hideContextView()`
1. This disposable gets called which immediately closes the context menu https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/5eb1fa759598db6c69f39331506e7222aecf59e5/src/vs/base/browser/ui/contextview/contextview.ts#L364

Probably related to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/32f0bc498bb20a6e3a09a979a6e055af0b55b684

There's currently inconsistent keyboard navigation capabilities for certain menu items in the add context quick pick. When inside a sub menu, click the escape key will navigate back to the main menu, while other times the entire quick pick will be closed.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->

Open it in the chat customization view:

[bug, verified, candidate, chat-subagents] UI Bug: To small "Allow" button to be able to select the additional options when asked by sub-agent. Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No Version: 1.109.0-insider (user setup) Commit: 2c357a926df65ab880d7f79733074365163f269f Date: 2026-01-15T05:02:22.042Z Electron: 39.2.7 ElectronBuildId: 12953945 Chromium: 142.0.7444.235 Node.js: 22.21.1 V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Have a "Main" Agent that only can delegate tasks to (custom) sub-agents 2. sub-agents asks for executing a command in terminal 3. Cannot click the advanced context menu of the button to for example "Allow all commands in this session" etc. to be choosen I switched to a "Conductor" Agent Pattern, where the Main Agent delegates all tasks to sub-agents and therefor Main agent never asks for allowing execution of a command. Now that sub-agents asks for this, the UI is to small to pick the extended options. We can still click the normal "Allow" option by clicking the button, but we need then to click this everytime. This happens wheter chat is in extra windows or docked as a sidebar. Fast solution is to adjust the display of the button. Advanced solution would be, that somewhere the button for allowing all commands in this session etc. is allready present in at footer/header or somewhere else, so that even before the confirmation will come first time, the user can already pick the options (eg. already YOLO mode at the beginning ;D).

title: Agent issue with terminal tool
user_feedback: [Problem] Agent fails to detect command completion in terminal, [Symptoms] Agent waits for echos before proceeding and sends successive echos prematurely, [Environment] macOS 25.3.0 on Apple M4 with VS Code Insiders 1.113.0, [Possible Category] Terminal, [Problem Type] Command completion detection
retrieved_list:
[bug, *duplicate, chat-terminal] Copilot agent reports premature command completion (~10s timeout) while commands still running in terminal

## Environment
- Plugin: GitHub Copilot for Visual Studio Code 1.352.1715
- Editor: Visual Studio Code 1.103.0-insider
- Operating System: Darwin 24.5.0 (x64)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude 4.0 Sonnet
- Shell: bash 5.2.15 and zsh 5.9
- Terminal: VS Code integrated terminal

## Issue Description
GitHub Copilot agent incorrectly reports command completion for long-running commands while they are still executing in the terminal.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a conversation with GitHub Copilot agent
2. Request execution of a longer-running command like `npm run format` or `npm run lint`
3. Observe that the chat state changes to `waiting` briefly
4. After approximately 10 seconds, Copilot declares the command completed successfully
5. Check the actual terminal - the command is still running

## Expected Behavior
- Copilot should wait for actual command completion before reporting success
- Should accurately detect real command exit status
- Should capture complete command output

## Actual Behavior
- Copilot reports premature command completion (~10 seconds timeout)
- Shows "success" status while command still executing
- May miss actual command failures that occur after the timeout

## Technical Details Attempted
### Shell Integration Configuration
- Manually configured VS Code shell integration in `~/.bashrc`
- Added shell integration path: `$(code --locate-shell-integration-path bash)`
- Resolved `noclobber` conflicts that were preventing shell integration
- Confirmed `$TERM_PROGRAM=vscode` and shell integration variables are set
- Terminal decorations enabled: `"terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.decorationsEnabled": "both"`

### Commands Tested
- `npm run format` (ESLint + remark, ~30-60 seconds)
- `npm run lint` (ESLint only, ~15-30 seconds) 
- `npm run build` (Vite build, variable duration)
- `git log --oneline -10` (shorter command, works correctly)

### Shells Tested
- bash 5.2.15 with manual shell integration
- zsh 5.9 with shell integration
- Both exhibit same premature completion behavior

### VS Code Settings Applied
```json
{
 "terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled": true,
 "terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.decorationsEnabled": "both", 
 "terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.showCommandGuide": true,
 "terminal.integrated.stickyScroll.enabled": true,
 "terminal.integrated.enablePersistentSessions": true,
 "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.osx": "bash"
}
```

## Workaround Attempts
1. **Enhanced npm scripts with completion echo**: Added `&& echo '✅ Command completed successfully'` to scripts - still reports early completion
2. **Explicit shell integration setup**: Manually configured shell integration - no improvement
3. **Different shell configurations**: Tested bash and zsh - same issue
4. **Command chaining**: Used `command && echo "DONE"` pattern - Copilot still times out early

## Expected Fix
Copilot agent should:
1. Properly integrate with VS Code's shell integration to detect real command completion
2. Respect actual command exit codes and timing
3. Wait for shell integration's command completion signals rather than using arbitrary timeouts
4. Handle commands that take longer than 10 seconds without false positive completion

## Impact
This affects developer workflow when using Copilot agent for:
- Build processes
- Linting/formatting operations 
- Test suites
- Any command taking longer than ~10 seconds

The premature completion reporting makes it difficult to rely on Copilot agent for longer development tasks.

## Shell Configuration Files

### ~/.bashrc (Enhanced with VS Code shell integration)
```bash
# ---------- ~/.bashrc ----------
# interactive only
case $- in *i*) ;; *) return ;; esac

# oh-my-bash
export OSH='/usr/local/share/oh-my-bash'
export THEME="$HOME/.bash/themes/agnoster-bash/agnoster.bash"
[[ -f $THEME ]] && { export DEFAULT_USER=$(whoami); source "$THEME"; }
[[ -f "$OSH/oh-my-bash.sh" ]] && source "$OSH/oh-my-bash.sh"

# VS Code Insiders integration - silence notarisation error
codei() { command code-insiders "$@" 2> >(grep -v 'SecCodeCheckValidity' >&2); }
alias code=codei

# completions / plugins
completions=(git composer ssh)
aliases=(general)
plugins=(git npm bashmarks)

# python alias
alias python=/usr/local/bin/python3

# GPG
export GPG_TTY=$(tty)

# PATH additions
export PATH="$HOME/.rd/bin:$PATH"

# nvm
export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion" ] && . "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion"

# cargo
[ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ] && . "$HOME/.cargo/env"

# electron-forge completion
EFCOMP="$HOME/Documents/git/lui/plat-lui-portal-1/node_modules/tabtab/.completions/electron-forge.bash"
[ -f "$EFCOMP" ] && . "$EFCOMP"

# local bin
[ -f "$HOME/.local/bin/env" ] && . "$HOME/.local/bin/env"

# VS Code shell integration - enhanced with better command detection
if [[ "$TERM_PROGRAM" == "vscode" ]]; then
 # Temporarily disable noclobber for shell integration
 _noclobber_was_set=false
 if [[ -o noclobber ]]; then
 _noclobber_was_set=true
 set +o noclobber
 fi
 
 # Get and source the shell integration script (try insiders first, then regular)
 _vscode_shim="$(command code-insiders --locate-shell-integration-path bash 2>/dev/null || command code --locate-shell-integration-path bash 2>/dev/null)"
 if [ -n "$_vscode_shim" ] && [ -f "$_vscode_shim" ]; then
 . "$_vscode_shim"
 fi
 
 # Restore noclobber if it was originally set
 if [ "$_noclobber_was_set" = true ]; then
 set -o noclobber
 fi
 
 # Enhanced command detection function
 _vscode_command_complete() {
 local exit_code=$?
 echo "Command completed with exit code: $exit_code" >&2
 return $exit_code
 }
 
 # Override common commands for better detection
 alias npm='command npm'
 alias git='command git'
 
 unset _vscode_shim _noclobber_was_set
fi
```

### Shell Environment Detection
- Both bash and zsh exhibit the same premature command completion behavior
- Shell integration appears to load correctly (`$TERM_PROGRAM=vscode` detected)
- VS Code shell integration path resolves successfully
- noclobber conflicts have been resolved in bash configuration

## Additional Context
- Copilot Diagnostics session ID: 7fbeafcd-f63e-40c0-95f9-d893589a5bd61754335618256
- Network connectivity: All endpoints reachable (HTTP 200)
- Content exclusion: enabled
- Restricted telemetry: disabled

## Logs

### How to Collect Logs
1. Open VS Code Command Palette (`Cmd+Shift+P`)
2. Type: `Developer: Show Logs`
3. Select relevant logs:
 - **Terminal** (for shell integration issues)
 - **GitHub Copilot** (for agent behavior)
 - **Extension Host** (for extension interactions)

### Key Log Locations
- VS Code Insiders logs: `~/Library/Application Support/Code - Insiders/logs/`
- Latest session: `20250804T152657` (or most recent timestamp)
- Look for entries during command execution timeframe

### Expected Log Entries to Look For
- Terminal shell integration initialization
- GitHub Copilot agent command execution start/stop
- Shell integration command completion signals
- Any timeout or premature completion messages

## Live Reproduction Example

### Actual Command Execution Log
```bash
WI1-1002265MBP:plat-lui-portal gabrielkohen$ echo "=== Starting npm run lint at $(date) ===" && npm run lint
=== Starting npm run lint at Mon Aug 4 17:46:07 EDT 2025 ===

> @by/plat-lui-portal-mui5@2.9.8 lint
> node ./.github/scripts/lint.js && echo '✅ Lint completed successfully'

Starting linting
# ❌ OUTPUT STOPS HERE - Copilot agent reports "success" but command still running
# ❌ Missing: ESLint output, remark output, completion echo message
# ❌ Timeline: ~10 seconds after start, agent declares victory prematurely
```

### Evidence of Premature Completion
- **Start time**: `Mon Aug 4 17:46:07 EDT 2025`
- **Expected duration**: 15-30 seconds for full lint process
- **Agent timeout**: ~10 seconds after start
- **Missing output**: ESLint results, remark results, completion echo
- **Expected end marker**: `✅ Lint completed successfully` (never shown)

This demonstrates the exact issue: Copilot agent reports command completion while the actual lint process is still running in the terminal.

## Terminal Session Context
- Commands tested: `npm run lint`, `npm run format` 
- Shell: bash 5.2.15 and zsh 5.9
- Issue occurs consistently around 10-second mark
- Shell integration appears active (`$TERM_PROGRAM=vscode`)

## Reproduction Repository
Repository: BY-Product-Development/plat-lui-portal
Branch: feature/LUI-28750-fix-screenshot-capture-on-availability-test-failures
Commands available for testing: `npm run lint`, `npm run format`, `npm run build`

 ## Issue
@bpasero I've noticed that when I use keyboard shortcuts to focus sidebar views, the main window isn't automatically focused. 

For example, say I have two windows, the main window and an aux window:

- If, in the aux window editor, I highlight some text and `Ctrl + Shift + H` (replace in files), the search view is revealed but an `Alt + Tab` to the main window is necessary before I can use my keyboard to interact with the search view.

- Similarly, if in the aux window editor, I use the "reveal active file in explorer view" command, the explorer view is revealed with the file highlighted, but an `Alt + Tab` to the main window is required before I can use my keyboard to interact with the explorer view.

Would it make sense to automatically focus the main window when a sidebar view receives focus?

Testing #247399

There's currently inconsistent keyboard navigation capabilities for certain menu items in the add context quick pick. When inside a sub menu, click the escape key will navigate back to the main menu, while other times the entire quick pick will be closed.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open add context quick pick
2. Using keyboard navigate to "Files & Folders..." and click enter
3. Once sub menu is visible click escape key
4. Should navigate back to main menu
5. Now navigate to "Instructions..." and click enter
6. Once sub menu is visible click escape key

This final escape key press will close the quick menu when I would've expected to go back to the main menu. This is an issue for "Instructions..." and "Symbols..." menu items.
